translated: Welcome to Project SEKAI After Talk: Reaching Out to an Unraveling Tomorrow! Hello, everyone!
I'm Sato Hinata, the voice of Akiyama Mizuki from 25-ji, Nightcord de.
This After Talk is a stream where we look back on the event while reading the messages you've sent us.
This time, we're focusing on the 'Reaching Out to an Unraveling Tomorrow' event.
I hope you all enjoyed the story!
The event just ended yesterday. How was it for everyone?
What did you think?
I really hope you all enjoyed it.
It was a story where Mizuki really faced the future and thought about what they want to do,
and I think it was a very meaningful chapter.
For those of you who are at a stage where you have to decide on your education or career path, I think this story might have really resonated with you.
Starting in April, After Talk has been slightly renewed, and we've been collecting messages in advance via the official Discord and email.
Thank you so much to everyone who sent one in.
I'll be reading some of your messages during the show, so please look forward to that.
Also, if you want to share your thoughts on the event or this stream, please post using the hashtag #ProsekaAfterTalk.
I'm keeping an eye on the YouTube chat, and I'll check the hashtag later too,
so I'll try to pick up as many interesting comments as I can.
Looking at the chat, there are so many international comments! I'm looking for Japanese ones... Oh, 'The ribbon is cute' and 'Your hair matches Mizuki's!'
The stream might be a bit delayed, but people are noticing my hair and outfit.
I always try to do this, but today I really dressed up with the card illustration in mind.
Hello, hello! Thank you, everyone.
Someone said, "The story and the new song were so good I cried my eyes out."
I'll talk about the song in a bit.
When I saw it at home, I was like, "What is this?!"
I mean, "What even is this?!"
I really felt the incredible effort the company put into it.
Yes, yes, yes.
I'll talk more about that later.
Now, let's dive right into the story of 'Reaching Out to an Unraveling Tomorrow.'
We've received messages about the story from all of you,
so I'd like to look back on it while reading them.
First, we have a message from Kotori no Mama.
"Hello, Hinata-san!"
Hello!
"My child and I both enjoy playing Project SEKAI."
"The Mizuki-chan banner was incredibly cute, funny, and moving."
"It was wonderful to see Mizuki-chan's various feelings through their past experiences and interactions with those around them."
"I think it's very difficult to stay true to yourself and not give up on what you love when you face obstacles."
"But I believe that if you hold onto those feelings, there will come a time when they are rewarded."
"Just like the members of Nightcord, I believe there are definitely people out there
who will see and accept you for who you truly are."
"I really love the stories in Project SEKAI."
"Hinata-san, thank you so much for always voicing Mizuki."
"I will continue to support both Mizuki-chan and Hinata-san with all my heart!"
Thank you so much, Kotori no Mama!
In this story, Mizuki's older sister comes back from overseas,
and it starts with her opening a pop-up shop. I felt it was a huge step forward for Mizuki to want the other Nightcord members to meet her sister.
Kanade and Mafuyu had met Yuuki-san before,
but Ena met her for the first time in this story, so the relationships are really progressing.
Above all, Mizuki loves making videos, but they also love
remaking clothes, visiting boutiques, and looking at makeup. They have so many things they love.
So, I think it's amazing that they're already facing the question of what they want to do for a career at this stage.
When I was looking at colleges, I just thought about studying something that would give me a specific skill for a job since I was paying for it, so I just went to tons of open houses.
My approach to finding what I liked was completely different from Mizuki's.
But I think this was a very important time for Mizuki to re-examine what they love at their own pace.
It wasn't just about the pop-up shop their sister opened;
realizing that their love for clothes isn't just a hobby, but something they might want to do professionally, is a huge deal. I'm just glad they could smile about it.
Alright, I've picked out a few scenes that I personally want to talk about, so let's take a look together.
Here it is.
It's so good.
It's just so good.
That's what I think.
Since I've already made my own career choices, seeing someone else reach that moment...
even if it's through witnessing it in an event story...
well, there are always moments where you wonder if you're doing the right thing.
But if you can look back 10 or 20 years from now and feel glad you made that choice,
then it was a good thing. And I think effort is what makes that possible.
I don't think there's a "wrong" choice if you put in the effort.
If you choose something and work hard at it, then in a year or two,
you might have moments where you regret it or think you shouldn't have done it.
But looking back after 10 or 20 years, I'm sure you'll see how it all connected.
You'll think, "If I hadn't done that then, I wouldn't be able to do this now."
Even if you're in a different profession, I think you'll feel that way. For those of you who are working hard now,
or have something you want to work hard at, I hope this event story gives you the courage to make that decision and keep going.
With that said, I'd like to talk about the illustrations next.
Let me read another message.
This one is from Meshi-meshi-san.
Thank you very much!
